principle: Preview the palette doing deck work. Never sell isolated swatches as proof.

background.label: Background
background.explanation: The main field behind a slide.

surface.label: Panel
surface.explanation: A second field for cards, text blocks, or sections.

muted_text.label: Secondary text
muted_text.explanation: Captions, notes, and less important copy.

accent_primary.label: Accent
accent_primary.explanation: The colour that interrupts or points.

accent_secondary.label: Second accent
accent_secondary.explanation: Use only when it has a different job.

on_accent.label: Text on accent
on_accent.explanation: Text placed inside the accent colour.

line_or_rule.label: Rules and dividers
line_or_rule.explanation: Boundaries that should not compete with text.

fitStatuses.meaning: The measured text fits at the intended size and spacing.
The measured text fits, but exceeds the editorial soft target or leaves little breathing room.
The measured text overflows at the minimum permitted size. The interface must show the overflow and ask for an edit.

templates.name: The cover
templates.job: Prove first-impression hierarchy and title legibility.

templates.name: The explanation
templates.job: Prove the system can carry ordinary narrative slides repeatedly.

templates.name: The voice
templates.job: Prove expressive type can still be read and attributed.

templates.name: The image plus argument
templates.job: Prove text, image, and accent can coexist without mud or theft.

templates.name: The evidence
templates.job: Prove values, labels, and data marks remain distinguishable.

fields.headline: What remains after the party leaves
fields.subheadline: A tender, difficult story about friendship, work, and the price of staying.
fields.body: The deck must hold still images, practical production detail, and an emotional argument without treating every slide like a poster.
fields.quote: We thought the night would make us free. It only made the choices visible.
fields.caption: Director's note

samplePacks.name: A useful thing asking to exist

fields.headline: A public tool for choosing color without pretending taste is science
fields.subheadline: Built for people making decks under real constraints: deadlines, bad projectors, too much copy, and no design department.
fields.body: The proposal needs to explain the problem, demonstrate the method, and make the work feel possible. Color supports the argument; it does not become the argument.
fields.quote: Make the useful decision visible, then let the person change it.
fields.caption: Working principle

samplePacks.name: A company with something concrete to prove

fields.headline: Infrastructure for the people already doing the invisible work
fields.subheadline: A practical system for small teams that have outgrown spreadsheets but not their common sense.
fields.body: The deck combines customer evidence, operating detail, market context, and a clear request. The palette must survive repetition and dense slides without becoming grey soup.
fields.quote: The work was always there. The software finally noticed it.
fields.caption: Customer interview, Austin

fields.headline: Make the next decision easier.
fields.subheadline: A clear recommendation, enough evidence to trust it, and room for an honest question.
fields.body: Put the recommendation where the room can see it. Keep the evidence close enough to trust, then move the furniture into the appendix.
fields.quote: If the point is hiding, the deck is not finished.
fields.caption: Working principle
